steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question. The audience already knows the Atlantic Monthly, so the correct choice must introduce Halverson's book effectively — that means focusing on what the book is about, not re-explaining a magazine the audience already knows.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Cathryn Halverson's Faraway Women and the "Atlantic Monthly" discusses female authors whose autobiographies appeared in the magazine in the early 1900s.: Names the book and describes its content (female authors, early-1900s autobiographies) without providing any background on the Atlantic Monthly. Perfectly suited for an audience already familiar with the magazine. ✓
  • Choice B — A magazine called the Atlantic Monthly, referred to in Cathryn Halverson's book title, was first published in 1857.: Introduces the Atlantic Monthly as if unknown to the audience and leads with the magazine's founding date rather than Halverson's book. This contradicts the audience's stated familiarity and fails to introduce the book. ✗
  • Choice C — Faraway Women and the "Atlantic Monthly" features contributors to the Atlantic Monthly, first published in 1857 as a magazine focusing on politics, art, and literature.: Provides founding-date background about the Atlantic Monthly — unnecessary for an audience already familiar with it — and does not effectively introduce the book's specific subject. ✗
  • Choice D — An author discussed by Cathryn Halverson is Juanita Harrison, whose autobiography appeared in the Atlantic Monthly in the early 1900s.: Mentions only one author example from the book instead of introducing the book's overall focus. This is too narrow to serve as an introduction to Halverson's work. ✗
A is correct.
